name: Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail
name_original: Nacimiento Río Cadagua — circular desde Lezana de Mena
description: Hike the easy 3.3-mile Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail to discover the river's source, waterfalls, and meadows in Valle de Mena.
region_intro: Hiking around Valle De Mena offers diverse landscapes within a mountainous amphitheater, bordered by the Montes de Ordunte, Montes de la Peña, and Sierra Salvada. The region features lush vegetation, including beech, holm oak, and pine trees, alongside extensive green pastures. Numerous water features, such as the source of the Cadagua River and several waterfalls, characterize the terrain. Hikers can explore varied environments, from riverbanks to mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Valle De Mena The most popular hiking route is Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This easy route leads to the source of the Cadagua River, featuring waterfalls and meadows. Another top favourite among local hikers is Forgotten Way and Ordunte Reservoir Loop, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail offers views of the Ordunte Reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Local hikers also love the Cascada de Aguasal y Monte de Naviciada — circular desde Laya, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past the Cascada de Aguasal, often completed in about 4 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Valle De Mena is defined by mountainous amphitheaters, lush landscapes, and abundant water features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding mountain climbs. The routes in Valle De Mena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Mena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valle De Mena?
A: Valle De Mena offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 11 easy, 13 moderate, and 6 difficult options to explore the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Valle De Mena?
A: Yes, Valle De Mena has 11 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047623828/nacimiento-del-cadagua-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3-mile route that leads to the source of the Cadagua River, featuring waterfalls and meadows.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Valle De Mena?
A: Absolutely. Valle De Mena is known for its abundant water features. You can explore trails leading to waterfalls such as Aguasal, El Hijuela, Peñaladros, and San Miguel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047219862/cascada-de-aguasal-y-monte-de-naviciada-circular-desde-laya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Aguasal y Monte de Naviciada — circular desde Laya&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you past the Cascada de Aguasal.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Valle De Mena?
A: Yes, many trails in Valle De Mena are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047723230/forgotten-way-and-ordunte-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forgotten Way and Ordunte Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047219862/cascada-de-aguasal-y-monte-de-naviciada-circular-desde-laya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Aguasal y Monte de Naviciada — circular desde Laya&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Valle De Mena?
A: Valle De Mena offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter lush vegetation including beech, holm oak, maple, pine, and oak trees, alongside extensive green pastures. The region is characterized by mountainous amphitheaters, riverbanks, and significant water features like the Cadagua River and Ordunte Reservoir. Higher trails offer panoramic views of the Montes de Ordunte, Montes de la Peña, and Sierra Salvada.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Valle De Mena?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Valle De Mena, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse terrain, from easy river walks to challenging mountain ascents, and the abundance of natural beauty, including waterfalls and lush forests.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Valle De Mena is rich in history and culture. Many hiking trails pass by Romanesque churches like San Lorenzo de Vallejo and Santa María de Siones. You can also find 15 preserved defensive towers scattered throughout the valley, and an ancient Roman road between Irús and Arceo features small waterfalls.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valle De Mena?
A: Valle De Mena is beautiful year-round, but the best time for hiking generally depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, with lush greenery in spring and rich hues in autumn. Summers are warm, ideal for enjoying water features, while winters can offer a different, quieter experience, especially in higher elevations.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or mountain peaks accessible by hiking in Valle De Mena?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Valle De Mena provides several opportunities for stunning views and peak ascents. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Pico del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/647793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zalama Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/538844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgüeno Summit&lt;/a&gt;. More challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047726298/mount-kolitza-and-mount-burueno-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Kolitza and Mount Burueño Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and panoramic vistas.

Q: What are some notable natural features to look out for on hikes?
A: Beyond the numerous waterfalls and the source of the Cadagua River, hikers can explore the perimeter trail of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1441487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ordunte Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, known as the "Camino del Calcolítico." The region's diverse forests, including beech, holm oak, and pine, also provide beautiful natural settings for exploration.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Valle De Mena?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points, especially near villages like Villasana de Mena or key natural attractions, offer designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details or local information for the best parking options for your chosen hike.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Valle De Mena using public transport?
A: While public transport options exist to reach the Valle De Mena region, direct access to specific trailheads can be limited. Villasana de Mena, the capital, serves as a central hub. For reaching more remote starting points, a car is often recommended for convenience and flexibility.
tips: In this stretch of road between the towns of Lezana de Mena and Sopeñano, the Río Cadagua crosses it, where it offers a large number of waterfalls that gradually descend in an eastward direction.

Beautiful long in the middle of nature and where the Río Cadagua is born in the middle of the Montes de la Peña. Depending on the time of year, this birth can be found more or less full of water and where sometimes, you have to go to look for the birth a few meters below.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for the Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail is approximately 5.3 kilometers (3.3 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 30 minutes to complete. However, depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the sights, it could take a bit longer.

faqs.question: Is the Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered easy, making it suitable for beginners and families. It has a low to low-medium difficulty, with manageable elevation gain. If you can comfortably walk up gentle slopes and navigate uneven terrain, you'll find this route enjoyable.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Nacimiento del Cadagua itself, the impressive karstic spring that is the source of the Cadagua River. You'll also encounter the beautiful Waterfalls of the Cadagua River, especially after rainfall. The trail also passes through charming agricultural landscapes and may include historical sites like Romanesque churches depending on the specific loop chosen.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a pleasant experience. You'll walk on paths winding through meadows, country tracks, and sections with streams. Near the river's source, expect mossy areas and natural pools. It's generally well-maintained but can be uneven in places.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for the Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail?
faqs.answer: Common starting points for routes exploring the Nacimiento del Cadagua include Leciñana de Mena, Cadagua, or Vallejo de Mena. You'll typically find parking available in or near these villages, which serve as convenient access points to the trail network.

faqs.question: Is the Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs aren't detailed, trails in this region are generally dog-friendly. It's always best practice to keep your dog on a leash, especially around wildlife, other hikers, and livestock in agricultural areas. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but the spring and autumn months are particularly pleasant for hiking, with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. If you want to see the Waterfalls of the Cadagua River at their most impressive, visit after periods of significant rainfall when the karstic spring is fully recharged.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in Las Merindades?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail or other public trails within the Las Merindades region. The area is freely accessible for outdoor recreation.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Cuenca del Cadagua, Camino de Las Merindades, Ruta de los Sentidos, Camino Herrería, Camino Lavadero, and even a small portion of the historic GR-85.

faqs.question: Are there any restaurants or cafes near the Nacimiento del Cadagua Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the Valle de Mena, which has several charming villages. You can find local restaurants and cafes in nearby towns like Leciñana de Mena, Cadagua, or Vallejo de Mena, perfect for a meal or refreshment before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this easy trail, comfortable hiking shoes are recommended, especially as some sections can be uneven. Bring water, snacks, and appropriate clothing for the weather conditions. A camera is also a good idea to capture the beautiful scenery, especially the Source of the Cadagua.
